3281600.name: Eyes on Yuki!
3281600.description:
Ever babysitted a magical little frog with the IQ of a toaster and the energy of a squirrel after five espressos? Meet Yuki: an adorable creature that is both the origin and the solution to this mess.

{STEAM_APP_IMAGE}/extras/yukimainsequence_

Eyes on Yuki is a co-op tower defense where you must protect shrines from hordes of enemies while having Yuki around to purify them.

See the thing is that Yuki is not the brightest crayon in the box, so if you want to keep it close you must keep your Eyes on Yuki (see what I did there?)

🎮 Coop Tower Defense

Tired of defending towers all alone? Well now there's two of you! It can be a little overwhelming at times, but what's teamwork without a little bit of chaos? You'll manage just fine.

{STEAM_APP_IMAGE}/extras/shotturrets_

🛡️ Is this a Tower Defense?

You’ll gather resources, exploit a cute little family of moles to build turrets and defend statues. So technically it is a Statue Defense, but yeah.

🌿 Gather Resources

Carry sap and shiny magic metals across the map. Some materials need two players—teamwork or tantrums, your choice.

{STEAM_APP_IMAGE}/extras/materials_.gif

🔨 Build Turrets

Whack the helmeted little moles to create turrets. Simple. You've seen it before. Slightly ridiculous. Highly effective.

🗿 Defend the Statues

Protect the frog-ish moais as they purify the world. No pressure. Just the fate of everything.

{STEAM_APP_IMAGE}/extras/statue_

🐸 Babysitting 101: Basic guide to babysit a frog

Meet Yuki: a magical creature with the attention span of a soggy crouton. Yet somehow, he’s the only one who can purify the statues and save the world.

{STEAM_APP_IMAGE}/extras/yukibabysit_

This means that everytime Yuki wanders off, sleeps, covers everything in frog goo or watches TikTok... you'll have to stop everything you are doing and bring him back-

or you could stay trapped on an infinite loop of chaos and cuteness, your choice.

🎨 A Whimsical Cozy World in Living Color

The world of Eyes on Yuki is a patchwork of cozy landscapes and questionable logic. From curious critters to statues waiting to bloom. Because saving the world should look (and feel) absolutely delightful—even if it doesn’t always make sense.
